message:
fix(fr-command): BLMPOP/BZMPOP/LMPOP/ZMPOP numkeys+COUNT wording matches upstream [bd-frankenredis-blmpcount]

Four mpop-family commands had error wording divergences from
upstream Redis 7.2 t_list.c::lmpopGenericCommand and
t_zset.c::zmpopGenericCommand:

- numkeys with non-numeric input: fr returned generic
  'value is not an integer or out of range'; upstream emits
  'numkeys should be greater than 0' (used for both unparseable
  and non-positive).

- COUNT with non-numeric input: same divergence; upstream emits
  'count should be greater than 0'.

- BLMPOP COUNT 0 specifically returned 'COUNT value of 0 is
  not allowed'; upstream uses the generic 'count should be
  greater than 0'.

All four commands now share a uniform bad_numkeys() / bad_count()
closure that matches upstream byte-for-byte.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
